# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// src/middlewares/authMiddleware.ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next';  // Import the necessary types for handling requests in nextjs middle ware functions  
export function middleware(req: NextRequest) :NextResponse | Promise<NextResponse>{   
     const token = req.cookies['authToken'];      // Get auth Token stored as a cookie named 'authToken'  from the request object if it exists, otherwise return unauthenticated response  
      
        /* If no authentication tokens are present in cookies then redirect to login page */   
     if (!token) {        
          const url = req.nextUrl;      // Get URL of current Request and set a new path for redirection 
           url.pathname='/login'   ;// Set the nextURL as '/Login', this will be your redirected login page after user is not authenticated   
        return NextResponse.redirect(url);     /* Rediret to Login Page */      }       // Return a Promise that resolves with response object  or redirection URLs  
         else{         
             const url = req.nextUrl;           // Get the current request and set new path for redirecting   
              if (token !== process.env['NEXT_AUTH_SECRET']) {     /* Check token against secret key */ 
                  console.log('Invalid Token');      // If tokens do not match then log an error message  
                   url.pathname='/login' ;// Redirect to login page    }       return NextResponse.redirect(url);         }}          else{           /* User is authenticated */ 
                  console.log('User Authenticated');      // Log a success message if user has valid token  
                   url = new URL (req.nextUrl) ;// Create an instance of nextURL and set the path to '/'    return NextResponse.redirect(url);     }}          }  })       .use();       `;
